content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Both amendments identify genuine lifecycle reconciliation bugs confirmed by source code inspection. Amendment fr-11-677d73f5 describes a state-machine deadlock where resumable failed runs are permanently locked at the milestone layer. Amendment fr-11-25a6f69d describes a timestamp ordering defect that corrupts duration accounting. Both are real correctness issues that warrant fixes.

## Votes

- `fr-11-677d73f5`: Accept
  Verified all five code locations. handle_resume (run.rs:608) explicitly allows RunStatus::Failed through. sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options (run.rs:181-194) unconditionally maps every Failed run to MarkMilestoneFailed. The milestone state machine (model.rs:102) returns an empty transition target array for Failed. build_reconciled_transition_events has no handler for (Failed, _) so those transitions hit the default error branch. And bead starts are rejected for terminal milestones via is_terminal() check (service.rs:1800-1806). Together these create a concrete deadlock: a project that fails and is later successfully resumed will have its milestone permanently stuck in Failed with no recovery path. This is a real correctness bug in the failure-to-milestone mapping that needs to distinguish recoverable from terminal failures.
- `fr-11-25a6f69d`: Accept
  Verified all four code locations. update_task_run_with_disposition (service.rs:2074) and repair_task_run_with_disposition (service.rs:2204) both pass the run's started_at as the running_started_at parameter. The Paused->Failed bridge (service.rs:754-776) creates a synthetic Paused->Running event using bridge_timestamp derived from that started_at value. The NDJSON journal is read in append order by read_journal_from_path (fs.rs) with no chronological sorting, and accumulated_running_duration_seconds (service.rs:569-589) iterates in that append order. If an existing Running->Paused event at time T2 is followed by a synthetic Running event at earlier time T1, the duration calculation double-counts the overlap. This is a concrete data-integrity bug affecting both duration metadata and timeline monotonicity.
